data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: A distributed architecture where Netflix services are divided into small, independent units, each handling a specific function.
data.technical_spec: Deployed on AWS, uses Kubernetes for container orchestration.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A large-scale CDN setup to deliver streaming content efficiently across the globe.
data.technical_spec: Uses Open Connect, Netflix's custom CDN optimized for video streaming.

data.title: Cloud-Native Infrastructure
data.description: The complete infrastructure is hosted on the cloud for scalability and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Primarily relies on AWS services including EC2, S3, and Lambda.

data.title: Data-Driven Framework
data.description: Utilizes big data and analytics for decision-making in content recommendation.
data.technical_spec: Emploiys technologies like Apache Spark and Kafka.

data.title: Global DNS Traffic Management
data.description: Manages user requests globally to route them to the best performing datacenter.
data.technical_spec: Uses proprietary and third-party DNS services for traffic latency optimization.
